candidates.why: Represents the company’s main operating revenue; clearly the top-line sales figure.
Financial income, not operating revenue; should not be used for total revenue.
Component of financial income, not total revenue.
Net non-operating result, not revenue.
Includes net income plus other comprehensive income; not revenue.
Balance sheet asset, not total revenue line.
Liability representing customer prepayments, not revenue line.
General operating/overhead expenses, not clearly direct cost of goods or services.
Likely operating/overhead; could include some direct costs but label is too generic for pure COGS.
Selling/marketing expense, part of SG&A, not COGS.
Residual operating expenses; more SG&A-like than COGS.
Financing costs, not production/service costs.
Non-operating by definition; not COGS.
One-off items; may include various categories, not specifically COGS.
Asset account; not an income statement expense line.
Liability; not a direct expense line and not specifically COGS.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. The candidate list does not include the clearly appropriate COGS proxy. 'Purchase of Goods and Services' is the most direct representation of cost of goods/services, while the listed candidates are more general operating or non-operating expenses. Therefore I selected 'Purchase of Goods and Services' from the full A-column and flagged this for manual review. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Purchase of Goods and Services

candidates.why: Generic operating overhead; fits SG&A definition and is not labeled as a total.
Subcomponent of other operating expenses; overhead in nature, appropriate as SG&A component.

candidates.row_label: Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles - Gross
candidates.why: Balance sheet asset, not period SG&A expense; should be excluded.
Liability representing unpaid expenses; not an income statement SG&A component itself.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Amortization (D&A) of capitalized R&D; instructions say to exclude depreciation/amortization from SG&A components.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles
candidates.why: Amortization expense; should be excluded from SG&A per instructions even if classified in SG&A.

candidates.row_label: Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles - Accumulated Amortization & Impairment
candidates.why: Balance sheet contra-asset; not an SG&A period expense line.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. Selected multiple operating overhead components consistent with SG&A: payroll, management remuneration, other operating expenses, external third-party expenses, and advertising. Explicit amortization and depreciation lines are excluded per instructions, as are totals/subtotals. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Payroll and related expences, [Income Statement] External Expenses - Third Parties - Other, [Income Statement] Advertising Expense, [Income Statement] Management Remuneration

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Amortization of previously capitalized R&D; related to R&D but not the total current-period R&D expense.
Balance sheet asset balance; not the period expense.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Costs - Accumulated Amortization & Impairment
candidates.why: Accumulated amortization on the balance sheet; not the period expense.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Expense - Expensed & Capitalized - Total - Supplemental
candidates.why: Explicitly labeled as total R&D expense (expensed and capitalized); best match for R&D expense input.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Costs - Net - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing entry for net R&D asset; not the expense line.

final_choice.row_label: Research & Development Expense - Expensed & Capitalized - Total - Supplemental

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
candidates.why: Explicit interest expense line; primary choice for interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest component of lease liabilities; appropriate secondary interest expense line if primary is missing.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; tertiary fallback for interest expense.
Equity attributable to minority owners; not interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests; not interest expense.
Liability for interest incurred but unpaid; not the expense line itself.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Provided a priority list of interest expense labels; later processing can take the first non-missing value per year.

candidates.why: Only a component of equity, not total equity attributable to owners.
Total equity attributable to shareholders (before non-controlling interests); ideal for book equity (BE).
Represents total equity including non-controlling interests; would double count with MIB if used for BE.
Technical balancing line, not the substantive equity figure.
Represents total assets = equity + liabilities; not equity alone.

variables.notes: TOTAL EQUITY appears to represent equity attributable to the company’s shareholders, excluding non-controlling interests, which are listed separately.

candidates.why: Standard label for minority interest in equity; best choice for MIB.
Duplicate representation of non-controlling interests; using both would double count, so one line is sufficient.

variables.notes: Chose a single line, 'Non-controlling interests', to avoid double counting since 'Minority Interest' appears to be a duplicate line with the same values.
